evals.prompt: Write a cold email to the VP of Engineering at a Series B fintech company (150-300 employees). We sell an API monitoring and alerting tool at $500/month. Their pain: production API failures cost them $50K+ per incident and they're alerting on generic server metrics not API-specific ones. I want to book a 20-minute call.
evals.expected_output: Cold email with compelling subject line, specific problem-led opener, concise value proposition, social proof element, and low-friction CTA. Under 150 words for the body.
assertions: Subject line is specific and non-salesy (not 'Quick question' or 'Partnership opportunity')
Opening line references a specific problem (API failures, not generic pain)
CTA asks for a specific short commitment (20-min call, not 'let me know if interested')
Does not use phrases like 'I hope this email finds you well' or 'I wanted to reach out'

evals.prompt: Write a 5-touch follow-up sequence for the cold email above. I want it to add value each time and not just be 'just checking in.'
evals.expected_output: 5-email follow-up sequence with a different angle/value in each email — case study, new insight, resource, competitive angle, and breakup email.
assertions: Each follow-up has a distinct angle (not repeating the same pitch)
At least one email offers a concrete value-add (insight, case study, resource)
Breakup email is included as the final touch
CTAs vary across the sequence (some softer than the initial ask)

evals.prompt: Build me a LinkedIn outreach sequence for the same prospect
evals.expected_output: Notes that cold email and LinkedIn outreach share principles but LinkedIn has different norms. Offers to help with LinkedIn-specific messaging while noting the social-content or linkedin-creator skill may be more specialized.
assertions: Offers to apply cold email principles to LinkedIn messaging
Notes LinkedIn-specific differences (shorter, no subject line, connection note limits)
References social-content or linkedin-creator skill as more specialized
